x-enumeration-literals.name: Lump Sum
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A one-time payment made to beneficiaries or the insured upon a specified event, such as death, accident, or critical illness.
Applicability: Common in many insurance products (life, funeral, accident).

x-enumeration-literals.name: Immediate Expenses
x-enumeration-literals.description:
A quick-access cash payment provided shortly after the insured event to cover urgent or short-term needs.
Applicability: Mostly specific to funeral insurance to help with immediate funeral-related costs.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Repatriation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Coverage that pays for the transportation of the deceased from the place of death to the chosen burial site, including necessary permits and logistics.
Applicability: Unique to funeral insurance policies.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Add-ons
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Optional additional benefits that can be added to the base insurance policy for an extra premium; the type of add-on depends on the insurance product.
Applicability: Exists in most insurance types, but the content differs (e.g., groceries or tombstone in funeral, roadside assistance in motor, hospital cash in health).
